2012-04-01.title: Knee Surgery for Lin, a Title for Marbury
2012-04-01.abstract: Jeremy Lin, the acclaimed New York Knicks point guard, will undergo knee surgery this week and miss the rest of the regular N.B.A. season. Meanwhile, a former point guard for the Knicks, the two-time All-Star Stephon Marbury, led the Beijing Ducks to the national title in China. He scored 41 points in the final, which was watched by an estimated 80 million people.

2012-04-01.title: Pasty-gate and a Series of ‘Own Goals’ Hit Cameron
2012-04-01.abstract: It has been one of those weeks for Prime Minister David Cameron of Britain. A string of embarrassing gaffes includes a growing furor over the government’s tax assault on one of the country’s favorite snacks.

2012-04-01.title: Insurgents’ Bombs in Thailand: Bigger and Deadlier
2012-04-01.abstract: A string of orchestrated explosions rocked southern Thailand over the weekend, killing 14 people, injuring hundreds and raising fears of larger and deadlier bombs being used by suspected Muslim insurgents.

2012-04-01.title: From Prisoner to Parliament in Myanmar
2012-04-01.abstract: The democracy advocate Daw Aung San Suu Kyi assumed a new role in her country’s political transition.

2012-04-01.title: U.S. Joins Effort to Equip and Pay Rebels in Syria
2012-04-01.abstract: The United States and dozens of other countries, meeting on Sunday in Istanbul, moved closer to direct intervention in the Syrian conflict.

2012-04-02.title: Your Choice for World Bank President?
2012-04-02.abstract: The United States has been able to say who will lead the World Bank since its creation in 1944. But the world has changed since then. Is it time for the Bank to change too?

2012-04-02.title: Britain Sees Chechen Plot to Kill Exile
2012-04-02.abstract: According to court documents, “a henchman” of Chechnya’s president is accused of seeking to assassinate a rival politician in London.

2012-04-02.title: In Egyptian Hard-Liner’s Surge, New Worries for the Muslim Brotherhood
2012-04-02.abstract: Hazem Salah Abu Ismail’s old-school Islamism conflicts with the pragmatic politics of the Muslim Brotherhood, which is fielding its own Egyptian presidential candidate.

2012-04-02.title: Myanmar Elections
2012-04-02.abstract: The party of Daw Aung San Suu Kyi, the Burmese dissident, declared that she had won a seat in Myanmar’s Parliament on Sunday.

2012-04-02.title: North Korea Party Calls for Leadership Meeting
2012-04-02.abstract: The Workers’ Party will convene a conference on April 11, where the new leader, Kim Jong-un, is expected be anointed as the official head of the party.

2012-04-03.title: Iran: Hyundai Motor Ends Operations
2012-04-03.abstract: The Hyundai Motor Company, the automaking subsidiary of Hyundai, the South Korean conglomerate, has ended its business dealings with Iran, where it had extensive operations.

2012-04-03.title: France: Deportations of ‘Radicals’ Begin
2012-04-03.abstract: French authorities on Monday deported two foreign citizens they identified as “Islamist radicals” and said three others would soon be expelled.

2012-04-03.title: China: Nobel Winners Urge Tibet Talks
2012-04-03.abstract: Twelve Nobel Peace laureates have written a letter to President Hu Jintao of China, urging him to engage in “meaningful dialogue” with the Dalai Lama to address Tibetans’ frustrations.

2012-04-03.title: As Riots Boil Over, These Guards Don’t Budge
2012-04-03.abstract: Protests over Greece’s austerity measures have happened yards away from the Presidential Guard at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier, complicating the soldiers’ ceremonial work.

2012-04-03.title: Swiss Seek Arrest of 3 German Tax Officials Over Stolen Banking Data
2012-04-03.abstract: A deal for Switzerland to tax and fine secret German accounts appears in jeopardy amid continuing tensions over tax issues and a CD of stolen data German officials bought in 2010.

2012-04-04.title: The Building ‘Hillary 2016’ Wave
2012-04-04.abstract: The U.S. secretary of state denies that she’s interested in higher office, but no one seems to believe her, maybe not even her husband.

2012-04-04.title: Deal Close on Night Raids, U.S. and Afghan Officials Say
2012-04-04.abstract: The deal would give Afghan authorities legal oversight of nighttime raids on Afghan homes, while allowing American forces to retain a guiding role.

2012-04-04.title: Taliban Attacks Kill 9 Afghan Police Officers
2012-04-04.abstract: Fourteen officers are also missing after two days of insurgents’ attacks, the deadliest of which happened Monday at a police post in Helmand Province.

2012-04-04.title: Getting Personal About Aung San Suu Kyi
2012-04-04.abstract: A new interview with the British journalist Peter Popham more fully illuminates Daw Aung San Suu Kyi’s personal and political lives in Myanmar, adding context and dimensions beyond the usual profile of a self-sacrificing opposition leader shuttered away in a crumbling villa.

2012-04-04.title: Court Rejects Palestinians in Their Bid for a Tribunal
2012-04-04.abstract: For the Palestinian Authority, which had sought an investigation of an Israeli military offensive, the ruling was a setback to its effort to gain international recognition as a state.

2012-04-05.title: News Roundup: Deal on Night Raids, Marines in Australia, Iran’s Influence on Afghanistan
2012-04-05.abstract: A deal seems close on night raids in Afghanistan, United States Marines arrive in Australia, and American officials see a pattern of meddling by Iran in Afghanistan.

2012-04-05.title: Pinning Scottish Independence on a Fervor for 1314
2012-04-05.abstract: Alex Salmond, Scotland’s first minister, wants a referendum in 2014, counting on a surge in nationalist feelings stirred by memories of the Battle of Bannockburn against the English in 1314.

2012-04-05.title: A Presidential Race Leaves French Muslims Feeling Like Outsiders
2012-04-05.abstract: Even in Marseille, a city renowned for its tolerance, Muslims lament that when they are spoken of at all, they are referred to in divisive terms.

2012-04-05.title: Libya: Court Demands Handover of Qaddafi Son for Trial at Hague
2012-04-05.abstract: Judges at the International Criminal Court ordered Libyan authorities to surrender Seif al-Islam el-Qaddafi and send him to the court in The Hague “without further ado.”

2012-04-05.title: Maid’s Cries Cast Light on Child Labor in India
2012-04-05.abstract: A 13-year-old girl who worked as a maid reportedly led a life akin to slavery, in a symptom of India’s growing middle class and its demand for domestic workers, jobs often filled by children.

2012-04-06.title: Italian Populist Party Leader Who Once Backed Berlusconi Quits, Ending an Era
2012-04-06.abstract: Umberto Bossi — who founded the conservative, anti-immigrant Northern League — was famous for his cigars, white tank-tops and salty language.

2012-04-06.title: Loyalty to Syrian President Could Isolate Hezbollah
2012-04-06.abstract: Though it remains strong, Hezbollah could find itself caught up in a sectarian war between Iran, the region’s Shiite power, and Saudi Arabia, a protector of Sunni interests.

2012-04-06.title: Ramp? Mall? Ship? No, a Titan’s New Tomb
2012-04-06.abstract: The 17-story-tall white-tiled mausoleum being built to hold the remains of Simón Bolívar looks to many people here like, among other things, the world’s biggest skateboard ramp.

2012-04-06.title: $30,000 Watch Vanishes Up Church Leader’s Sleeve
2012-04-06.abstract: An opulent watch — a source of public embarrassment — disappeared from the wrist of Kirill I, patriarch of the Russian Orthodox Church, in a doctored photo.

2012-04-06.title: Emirates Detain Pair From U.S.-Backed Group
2012-04-06.abstract: The United Arab Emirates detained two employees of the National Democratic Institute, a pro-democracy organization, and barred one of them from leaving the country.

2012-04-07.title: A Populist’s Downfall Exposes Ideological Divisions in China’s Ruling Party
2012-04-07.abstract: The ouster of Bo Xilai, the party chief in Chongqing, points to possibly the most serious division in the party elite since the leadership upheavals during the 1989 Tiananmen Square protests.

2012-04-07.title: Elan Steinberg Dies at 59; Led World Jewish Congress
2012-04-07.abstract: Mr. Steinberg brought what he called a new, “American style” assertiveness to the World Jewish Congress as its top executive.

2012-04-07.title: Obama Embraces National Security as Campaign Issue
2012-04-07.abstract: With the general election campaign about to start, President Obama is set to emphasize an issue that few Democratic candidates have made such a focus.

2012-04-07.title: Greek Statistician Under Scrutiny for Budget Estimates Before Euro Crisis
2012-04-07.abstract: Andreas Georgiou, who was recruited in 2010 to help get Greece’s famously unreliable public accounting in order, has become the target of a criminal investigation.

2012-04-07.title: Storm Continues After German Writer’s Poem Against Israel
2012-04-07.abstract: The controversy over a poem by Günter Grass showed no signs of subsiding, even after he said that he meant to criticize the government and not Israel as a whole.

2012-04-08.title: Showtime: A Japanese Star Is Ready for His Close-up
2012-04-08.abstract: There’s essentially one number that all of American baseball is buzzing about — and all of Japan, for that matter. That number is 97, as in miles per hour, the speed of the high-end fastballs thrown by the Japanese pitcher Yu Darvish. As pitches go, that’s about as fast as it gets, and Mr. Darvish, the son of an Iranian father and a Japanese mother, is set to make his first major league start on Monday.

2012-04-08.title: A Chance to Unfreeze the Standoff on the Glacier
2012-04-08.abstract: The scale of the avalanche disaster in Pakistan could well renew a debate about the costly deployments of Indian and Pakistani military units on the Siachen Glacier, a place so high, cold and forbidding that it is sometimes called “the third pole.” Leaders from both countries met Sunday in New Delhi, with the glacier as a possible talking point.

2012-04-08.title: U.S. Transfers Control of Night Raids to Afghanistan
2012-04-08.abstract: An agreement on raids that are deeply unpopular in Afghanistan would give legal oversight to Afghan forces, putting American troops in a supporting role.

2012-04-08.title: As Russian Real Estate Money Floods in, Could New York Go the Way of London?
2012-04-08.abstract: Rich foreigners have helped make real estate in central London all but inaccessible to most Brits. Can you imagine a Manhattan where even most well-off Americans couldn’t afford to buy?

2012-04-08.title: Indian and Pakistani Leaders Encourage Ties in a Rare Visit
2012-04-08.abstract: India’s prime minister, the host, said he would visit Pakistan, a trip that many analysts regard as instrumental to any major diplomatic breakthroughs between the countries.

2012-04-09.title: In Russia, Charges Are Dropped in Jail Death
2012-04-09.abstract: Investigators ruled that the statute of limitations had run out in the case of a physician charged with negligence after a lawyer under her care died in custody in 2009.

2012-04-09.title: Tradition and Boat Race Both Upended on the Thames
2012-04-09.abstract: The annual boat race between Oxford and Cambridge is no stranger to protests, but no one had ever seen anything like this before.

2012-04-09.title: Ex-K.G.B. Agent Said to Win South Ossetia Presidency
2012-04-09.abstract: Early voting results indicated Sunday that Leonid Tibilov, a K.G.B. veteran, had been elected president of South Ossetia after a protracted election struggle that Moscow had hoped to avoid.

2012-04-09.title: America’s Place in the New World
2012-04-09.abstract: American leaders have long presumed that the world’s democracies will align themselves with the United States, but India and Brazil are challenging that idea.

2012-04-09.title: Indian and Pakistani Leaders Encourage Ties in a Rare Visit
2012-04-09.abstract: India’s prime minister will now visit Pakistan, a trip regarded as instrumental to any major diplomatic breakthrough between the countries.

2012-04-10.title: A Work-and-Save Project in Bangladesh: It Makes a Village
2012-04-10.abstract: A dozen women in the impoverished village of Someshpur, and more than 24,000 women elsewhere in Bangladesh, have recently become micro-entrepreneurs. They now fish, they now make compost, they now trade firewood — thanks to modest savings they accumulated during two years of work maintaining roads in the vicinity.

2012-04-10.title: In French Vote, Sound and Fury From the Left
2012-04-10.abstract: Jean-Luc Mélenchon draws large, passionate crowds, and with a field of 10, candidates of both the far right and left gain clout.

2012-04-10.title: Killing of Bangladeshi Labor Organizer Signals an Escalation in Violence
2012-04-10.abstract: Aminul Islam, who was found murdered outside Dhaka last week, was a leader who had fought against low wages in the country’s garment industry.

2012-04-10.title: Despite Warning, Old Handouts Die Hard for Greek Politicians Facing Voters Soon
2012-04-10.abstract: With elections looming, lawmakers are scrambling to push through costly amendments to bills intended to meet the demands of foreign creditors.

2012-04-10.title: Brazil and U.S. Accentuate the Positive
2012-04-10.abstract: The meeting between President Dilma Rousseff of Brazil and President Obama was friendly, but the two countries still do not agree on a range of important issues.

2012-04-11.title: Court Flips Egypt’s Timetable: Election, Then Constitution
2012-04-11.abstract: A ruling by an obscure administrative court has put an end to any chance that a new constitution would be drafted before the presidential election, raising the stakes in race.

2012-04-11.title: A Russian City Always on the Watch Against Being Sucked Into the Earth
2012-04-11.abstract: Berezniki, east of Moscow, is plagued by a peculiar, post-Soviet industrial catastrophe: large sinkholes that have been opening into an abandoned mine.

2012-04-11.title: Moscow Protesters Try to Expand Movement
2012-04-11.abstract: Prominent organizers of antigovernment protests in Moscow descended on a southern Russia city to take up the cause of an opposition mayoral candidate who claimed election fraud.

2012-04-11.title: Where Trip Began, Remembering Titanic’s Lost
2012-04-11.abstract: A hundred years after the ship left Southampton, the port city marked the anniversary of the epic disaster with a recording of the ship’s whistle.

2012-04-11.title: Iranian President Says Oil Embargo Won’t Hurt
2012-04-11.abstract: President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad of Iran proclaimed defiantly on Tuesday that the country had such plentiful reserves of money that it could survive for years without exporting any oil.

2012-04-12.title: Ahmed Ben Bella, Revolutionary Who Led Algeria After Independence, Dies at 93
2012-04-12.abstract: A man for whom life was combat, Mr. Ben Bella fought both for and against France, and in Algeria he went from being the president to being a prisoner.

2012-04-12.title: Surname of Ex-Official’s Wife Adds New Twist to Mystery
2012-04-12.abstract: The way Chinese party officials identified the wife of deposed leader Bo Xilai may hint at more trouble for her, experts suggest.

2012-04-12.title: Briton’s Wanderings Led Him to Heart of a Chinese Scandal
2012-04-12.abstract: Neil Heywood’s ties to Bo Xilai and his family may have cost him his life and set off China’s biggest political scandal in a generation. But precisely why, or how, he died is still unknown.

2012-04-12.title: In Poppy War, Taliban Aim to Protect a Cash Crop
2012-04-12.abstract: An attack on a Helmand Province police headquarters was part of the militants’ increased efforts to protect opium poppy farmers and their economic interests.

2012-04-12.title: Raymond Aubrac, an Exalted Leader of the French Resistance, Dies at 97
2012-04-12.abstract: Mr. Aubrac and his wife, Lucie, became symbols of heroism in their country’s fight against the Germans, who defeated France in 1940. Their story of valor and love was told in movies and books.

2012-04-13.title: Greece’s Right-Wing Fringe
2012-04-13.abstract: As Greece reels from deep austerity cuts and prepares for elections, some ultranationalist groups like the Golden Dawn Party are appealing to increasingly insecure voters.

2012-04-13.title: Hard Times Lift Greece’s Anti-Immigrant Fringe
2012-04-13.abstract: The gains by Golden Dawn show how the economic collapse has empowered fringe groups, a situation that some in Greece have compared to Weimar Germany.

2012-04-13.title: Pakistan Gives U.S. a List of Demands, Including an End to C.I.A. Drone Strikes
2012-04-13.abstract: The government and opposition issued a joint list of demands that could pave the way for the reopening of NATO supply lines there.

2012-04-13.title: Murder Aside, China Inquiry Puts Couple’s Wealth on Trial
2012-04-13.abstract: Gu Kailai, a lawyer, spent more than two decades turning the government posts of her husband, Bo Xilai, into lucrative business opportunities.

2012-04-13.title: Election Is a New Start for an Aide to Mubarak
2012-04-13.abstract: Omar Suleiman’s sudden re-emergence has spurred fear — and, in some precincts, hope — at the prospect of a new strongman in Egypt.

2012-04-14.title: Not Just Another Fake Mona Lisa
2012-04-14.abstract: A researcher at the Prado in Spain has discovered that the museum’s copy of the Mona Lisa was painted by someone working next to Leonardo da Vinci while he created the original.

2012-04-14.title: Beneath That Beguiling Smile, Seeing What Leonardo Saw
2012-04-14.abstract: A researcher at the Prado, using high-tech tools, discovered that a copy of the Mona Lisa was most likely painted by someone sitting next to Leonardo da Vinci as he produced his masterpiece.

2012-04-14.title: Party May Be Long-Term Loser in Chinese Scandal
2012-04-14.abstract: The accusations against the former official Bo Xilai and his wife may end up raising doubts among many Chinese.

2012-04-14.title: Seeking Nuclear Insight in Fog of the Ayatollah’s Utterances
2012-04-14.abstract: The C.I.A. is using the remarks of Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, to gain insight into the geopolitical gamesmanship now at play over Iran’s nuclear program.

2012-04-14.title: Resurgent Beirut Offers Haven Amid Turmoil of Arab Spring
2012-04-14.abstract: For Lebanese content to stay out of the storm, and for Arab visitors seeking respite from fear and uncertainty around the region, Beirut is a destination to enjoy life.

2012-04-15.title: NATO Sees Flaws in Air Campaign Against Qaddafi
2012-04-15.abstract: The findings undercut the idea that NATO could effectively carry out a more complicated campaign in Syria without relying disproportionately on the United States military.

2012-04-15.title: Avoiding Another Tick on the Doomsday Clock
2012-04-15.abstract: It’s still 5 minutes to midnight. The failed North Korean missile launching on Friday has not yet moved back the minute hand on the Doomsday Clock.

2012-04-15.title: Complex Attack by Taliban Sends Message to the West
2012-04-15.abstract: Suicide bombers and gunmen struck the Afghan capital and three provinces in a clear attempt to erode confidence in NATO and Afghan military gains.

2012-04-15.title: North Korean Leader Stresses Need for Strong Military
2012-04-15.abstract: Kim Jong-un’s first public speech came two days after a North Korean rocket carrying a satellite disintegrated in midair.

2012-04-15.title: Lapses in Cease-Fire Reported as U.N. Monitors Prepare to Begin Mission in Syria
2012-04-15.abstract: United Nations cease-fire monitors were due to arrive in Syria on Sunday even as activists described violence erupting in Homs and an area near the Turkish border.

2012-04-16.title: President Speaks Out on Guard Investigation
2012-04-16.abstract: President Obama said he would be “angry” if it turned out that Secret Service agents had solicited prostitutes before a summit in Colombia.

2012-04-16.title: Declining as a Manufacturer, Japan Weighs Reinvention
2012-04-16.abstract: Many Japanese have a sense that their country has outgrown an economic template based on constructing commodity goods, but they disagree over what should replace it.

2012-04-16.title: Americas Meeting Ends With Discord Over Cuba
2012-04-16.abstract: Western Hemisphere nations failed to issue a consensus statement amid disagreements over whether to let Cuba join future summit meetings.

2012-04-16.title: Spanish King’s Hip Surgery Follows Controversial Hunting Trip
2012-04-16.abstract: King Juan Carlos, who was injured during a vacation to Botswana, has been called upon by some politicians to release details on his trip’s financing.

2012-04-16.title: Ai Weiwei on the Pen and the Gun
2012-04-16.abstract: The dissident artist Ai Weiwei has thrown down another bold challenge to the Chinese authorities with a new essay that assails Beijing over its suppression of dissent and censorship of the Internet. He writes, “The government computer has one button: delete.”

2012-04-17.title: Iran: Ready to Resolve Dispute, and Eager to Ease Sanctions
2012-04-17.abstract: Foreign Minister Ali Akbar Salehi said Monday that Iran was prepared to resolve the nuclear dispute with world powers at their next meeting, if the West eased the sanctions imposed on Iran.

2012-04-17.title: Greece: Ex-Official Is Detained
2012-04-17.abstract: Akis Tsochatzopoulos, a former defense minister, was detained Monday after testifying in connection with a money-laundering racket.

2012-04-17.title: Mali: Ancient Books Stolen
2012-04-17.abstract: The United Nations expressed alarm on Monday over the safety of ancient books and documents in the storied city of Timbuktu as reports said that rebels had pillaged and looted institutions.

2012-04-17.title: South Sudan: Blasts at U.N. Site
2012-04-17.abstract: The United Nations said Monday that one of its peacekeeping compounds in South Sudan sustained “material damage” over the weekend when it was hit with five bombs.

2012-04-17.title: Son’s Parties and Privilege Aggravate Fall of Elite Chinese Family
2012-04-17.abstract: Communist party officials are angered by the lifestyle of Bo Guagua, whose parents, Bo Xilai and Gu Kailai, are subjects of corruption and murder investigations.

2012-04-18.title: Each Side Accuses the Other of Breaking Syrian Cease-Fire
2012-04-18.abstract: The sides traded accusations as an advance team of United Nations observers, sent to monitor the cease-fire, was spotted in the southern city of Dara’a.

2012-04-18.title: Kurds Remain on the Sideline of Syria’s Uprising
2012-04-18.abstract: Syria’s Kurds have endured decades of marginalization, but they are still wary of getting involved in the country’s uprising.

2012-04-18.title: Britain Presses for Information on Death in China
2012-04-18.abstract: The British government is suddenly demanding more information in the case of Neil Heywood, a British businessman who was believed murdered in China last year and had ties to Bo Xilai’s family.

2012-04-18.title: Frenzied Hours for U.S. on Fate of a China Insider
2012-04-18.abstract: A Chinese official who said he had information about Bo Xilai sought and was denied asylum at an American Consulate in China, ensnaring the Obama administration in a politically sensitive episode.

2012-04-18.title: Egypt Panel Affirms Ban on 3 Candidates for President
2012-04-18.abstract: Citing narrow technical reasons, the presidential election commission confirmed the disqualification of the three most polarizing candidates.

2012-04-19.title: Canada: Request to Leave Guantánamo
2012-04-19.abstract: Omar Khadr has applied to be transferred to a prison in Canada from Guantánamo Bay to serve the remainder of his sentence for war crimes.

2012-04-19.title: Ireland Takes Up Bill on Abortion Access
2012-04-19.abstract: Legislation would carry out a 1992 court ruling that called for an exception to a constitutional ban if pregnancy threatened a woman’s life.

2012-04-19.title: Pentagon Sought to Stop Paper From Using Photos
2012-04-19.abstract: Whether to publish images of soldiers in Afghanistan posing with body parts was the subject of intense debate, one that the Pentagon weighed in on.

2012-04-19.title: Indian State Finds Itself in Tight Grip of Addiction
2012-04-19.abstract: A border state with a history of opium use, Punjab has a large and growing drug problem that many health officials complain government is not confronting.

2012-04-19.title: In Brazil and Elsewhere, Dismay at Argentina’s Nationalization Move
2012-04-19.abstract: Financial experts say that the decision to nationalize YPF and other policies were making Argentina a hemispheric outlier.

2012-04-20.title: China: Tibetans Set Themselves on Fire
2012-04-20.abstract: Two Tibetan men on Thursday set themselves on fire in the town of Barma in the mountainous area called Ngaba by Tibetans and Aba in Chinese, according to Tibet advocacy groups.

2012-04-20.title: Iran: Dispute Over Islands Grows
2012-04-20.abstract: Iran’s military escalated tensions with the United Arab Emirates on Thursday, warning the country’s leaders to cease their claims of sovereignty over three islands in the Persian Gulf.

2012-04-20.title: U.S. Expands Inquiry of Suspected Misconduct by Agents in Colombia
2012-04-20.abstract: An investigation into the behavior of Secret Service members has been widened to determine if the misconduct was confined to the 11 employees who were first tied to the scandal, according to an American official.

2012-04-20.title: Turmoil in Egypt Race Puts Spotlight on Panel’s Motives
2012-04-20.abstract: The disqualifications of three leading presidential candidates threw a spotlight on the country’s influential five-member election commission.

2012-04-20.title: A Spate of Teenage Suicides Alarms Russians
2012-04-20.abstract: President Dmitri A. Medvedev and experts warned that too much publicity surrounding the deaths might be contributing to a copycat effect.

2012-04-21.title: Bahrain’s Formula One Gala Not Going as Planned
2012-04-21.abstract: Tens of thousands of Shiite opposition groups protested on Friday, calling the race a public relations stunt that masks the monarchy’s failures to address political discontent.

2012-04-21.title: Sudan Says Military Evicts South’s Army From Oil Area
2012-04-21.abstract: The United States, the United Nations and the African Union all condemned South Sudan for sending troops into Heglig last week and bringing the two countries to the brink of war.

2012-04-21.title: In a Rowdy Democracy, a Dictator’s Daughter With an Unsoiled Aura
2012-04-21.abstract: Park Geun-hye, the daughter of a slain South Korean dictator, has put herself in position to win the country’s next presidential election.

2012-04-21.title: Suspected Sale by China Stirs Concern at White House
2012-04-21.abstract: The Obama administration says it believes that a Chinese manufacturer sold North Korea the chassis and other parts for a missile-transport vehicle, a senior official said.

2012-04-21.title: In Venezuela, a Shortage of Staples
2012-04-21.abstract: Venezuela is one of the world’s top oil producers, but shortages of staples are a chronic part of life.

2012-04-22.title: In Uprooting of Kurds, Iraq Tests a Fragile National Unity
2012-04-22.abstract: Facing attacks, Kurds are fleeing for Kurdish-majority areas amid a deteriorating relationship between Kurdish leaders and the Arab-dominated government in Baghdad.

2012-04-22.title: Already in Myanmar’s Reformation, an Impasse
2012-04-22.abstract: Some analysts believe Aung San Suu Kyi and other new members of Parliament in Myanmar are making a political misstep in refusing to take the official oath of office — not until a single problematic word is changed. In this, her first standoff with the government as an elected lawmaker, will she blink first, or will they?

2012-04-22.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-04-22.abstract: Top magazine and long-form articles this week.

2012-04-22.title: Hollande and Sarkozy Head to Runoff in French Race
2012-04-22.abstract: François Hollande, the Socialist challenger, came out on top in the first round of French presidential elections, but his lead over the incumbent, Nicolas Sarkozy, was slim.

2012-04-22.title: With Pact, U.S. Agrees to Help Afghans for Years to Come
2012-04-22.abstract: The draft of an agreement finalized on Sunday pledges American support for Afghanistan for 10 years after the withdrawal of troops at the end of 2014.

2012-04-23.title: For Results, Twitter Gets Out Early Word, in Code
2012-04-23.abstract: Twitter users in France on Sunday circumvented a law that prohibits the publication, by the press or any Internet-connected citizen, of presidential voting tallies or estimates before 8 p.m.

2012-04-23.title: Iranians Say They Took Secret Data From Drone
2012-04-23.abstract: An Iranian general said operations data about the aircraft that crashed in the country in December had been culled from its computer system, a claim discounted by American officials.

2012-04-23.title: Bahrain Holds Grand Prix, Keeping Protesters at Bay
2012-04-23.abstract: Protesters made no attempt to disrupt the race, other than shouting slogans. But in a nearby village, protesters set tires on fire, and some clashed with the police.

2012-04-23.title: Egypt Cancels the Delivery of Gas to Israel
2012-04-23.abstract: Israeli officials warned that the termination of natural gas shipments would cast a shadow over its peace treaty with Egypt.

2012-04-23.title: Newly Elected Myanmar Lawmakers Balk at Word in the Oath of Office
2012-04-23.abstract: Opposition members, led by Daw Aung San Suu Kyi, do not want to swear to “safeguard” a constitution that they had promised to rewrite in their campaigns.

2012-04-24.title: As China Official Rose, His Family’s Wealth Grew
2012-04-24.abstract: In the last decade, before Bo Xilai’s downfall, his family, including his and his wife’s siblings, quietly amassed a fortune estimated at more than $160 million.

2012-04-24.title: Hitting the Beach in Bangladesh
2012-04-24.abstract: A visit to Cox’s Bazar brings home the fact that Bangladesh, much like its flashier neighbors Thailand and Vietnam, is enjoying rapid economic growth. And though millions of Bangladeshis live in abject poverty, others have the cash to pay for some relaxation at this up-and-coming beachside destination.

2012-04-24.title: Chávez Out of Sight but Big as Life on Twitter
2012-04-24.abstract: The president of Venezuela remains in Cuba for cancer treatment, but keeps up a dialogue with political friends and foes that has led to a flurry of Internet rumors.

2012-04-24.title: Call for Growth to Counter German Push for Austerity
2012-04-24.abstract: A euro zone strategy to cut deficits has come under increasing strain from slowing economies, gyrating financial markets and electoral setbacks.

2012-04-24.title: French Far Right a Challenge for Europe and Sarkozy
2012-04-24.abstract: France’s president will need the support of the far right to win re-election, but the movement’s fickle voters may not deliver, and their growing numbers are a cause for alarm in the European Union.

2012-04-25.title: Israel Retroactively Legalizes 3 West Bank Settlements
2012-04-25.abstract: The provocative move, defended by the Netanyahu government as merely fixing technical issues, marked the first establishment of new settlements in two decades.

2012-04-25.title: Should the Privacy of a ‘Stalker’ Be Protected?
2012-04-25.abstract: An Olympic athlete’s punishment of a fan who sent a naked picture of himself to her sets off a storm in Germany over privacy rights. Some might ask, ‘Why?’

2012-04-25.title: Taking On Dementia With the Experiences of Normal Life
2012-04-25.abstract: A residential complex in the Netherlands that tries to help dementia patients continue to lead a normal life has received attention as a model.

2012-04-25.title: Battling Over Budget Cuts
2012-04-25.abstract: The brief truce between the Air Force and the Air National Guard over proposed budget cuts may already be unraveling in the Senate.

2012-04-25.title: Flamboyant Son of Fallen Chinese Leader Makes a Statement
2012-04-25.abstract: Bo Guagua, 24, a graduate student at Harvard, has been at the center of the remarkable downfall of his father, Bo Xilai, a former Politburo member. The son, who was famously said to have driven around Beijing in a red Ferrari, was even mentioned in the official statement about his father’s scandalous ouster. Through it all, the younger Mr. Bo has been silent. Until now.

2012-04-26.title: Pakistani Premier Faces Verdict in Clash With Court
2012-04-26.abstract: Prime Minister Yousaf Raza Gilani could lose his job and be imprisoned if convicted of contempt for refusing to revive an inquiry on his boss, President Asif Ali Zardari.

2012-04-26.title: Anger at Sarkozy Fuels Far-Right Party in France
2012-04-26.abstract: Supporters of the National Front and its presidential candidate, Marine Le Pen, say they resent President Nicolas Sarkozy’s efforts to win their votes as insincere.

2012-04-26.title: Prostitutes Perplexed as Glare Falls on City’s Brothels
2012-04-26.abstract: Cartagenans have struggled to come to terms with the commotion over the Secret Service scandal, but are also feeling anger and wounded national pride over an agent’s behavior.

2012-04-26.title: Life Inside a Brothel in Cartagena
2012-04-26.abstract: Though the recent scandal involving American Secret Service agents has cast new scrutiny on prostitution in Colombia, life in the brothels goes on as it always has.

2012-04-26.title: French Front-Runner Says He’d Seek to Renegotiate Fiscal Treaty if Elected
2012-04-26.abstract: There was little indication that Germany, the driving force behind the austerity-driven fiscal treaty, was warming to François Hollande’s ideas.

2012-04-27.title: The Netherlands: Budget Agreement Meets the European Union’s Targets
2012-04-27.abstract: The Netherlands defied expectations of a prolonged budget crisis on Thursday, rolling out a deal to meet European Union targets.

2012-04-27.title: Testifying, Murdoch Cites a ‘Cover-Up’ in Phone Hacking
2012-04-27.abstract: Rupert Murdoch, the chief of News Corporation, spread blame around in the hacking scandal, singling out former employees and others.

2012-04-27.title: In China, a Fall From Grace May Aid a Rise to Power
2012-04-27.abstract: With Bo Xilai out of power in China, Xi Jinping, the country’s presumed next leader, is emerging from the scandal with greater standing as China’s leadership transitions.

2012-04-27.title: Defense Minister Adds to Israel’s Recent Mix of Messages on Iran
2012-04-27.abstract: One day after Israel’s top general said the Iranian government was “rational,” his superior, Defense Minister Ehud Barak, said Thursday that the chances “appear low” that the country would halt its nuclear program.

2012-04-27.title: Taylor’s Rule in Liberia
2012-04-27.abstract: Charles G. Taylor, the former president of Liberia and once a powerful warlord, was convicted of abetting rebels who committed war crimes in Sierra Leone in the 1990s.

2012-04-28.title: Hollande and Sarkozy Trade Insults as Runoff Nears
2012-04-28.abstract: With a week to go before the incumbent Nicolas Sarkozy faces the Socialist candidate François Hollande in a runoff, the French presidential campaign has turned nastier.

2012-04-28.title: United States Talks Fail as Pakistanis Seek Apology
2012-04-28.abstract: Negotiations between the United States and Pakistan failed over Pakistani demands for an apology for American airstrikes last November that killed 24 Pakistani soldiers on the Afghan border.

2012-04-28.title: Raid to Kill Bin Laden Helped United States, Panetta Says
2012-04-28.abstract: In comments ahead of the first anniversary of the raid that killed Osama bin Laden, Defense Secretary Leon E. Panetta said that his death weakened Al Qaeda and made the United States more safe.

2012-04-28.title: Death of Spy, Zipped Into Bag, Spawns Theories and Inquest
2012-04-28.abstract: An inquest has brought forth details of the death in August 2010 of Gareth Williams, who was found locked in a duffel bag in a bathtub.

2012-04-28.title: Osama Bin Laden Joins the U.S. Presidential Race
2012-04-28.abstract: An April with the candidates already decided might slip into the political doldrums. Not this year. Before the Republicans define the president, the Obama camp is trying to remind voters he has accomplished some big things.

2012-04-29.title: Support From Islamists for Liberal Upends Race in Egypt
2012-04-29.abstract: Egypt’s most conservative Islamists backed a dissident ex-leader of the Muslim Brotherhood for president, confounding expectations about the dynamics of the Islamist movement.

2012-04-29.title: China Blocking All Mention of Chen and His Daring Escape
2012-04-29.abstract: China seems to have issued a gag order to prevent any mention of Chen Guangcheng and his daring late-night escape from house arrest. State-run newspapers have not yet carried any news about Mr. Chen, and Internet searches of his name, his initials and “blind man” are being blocked, along with the phrase “U.S. Embassy” — where Mr. Chen is said to have sought sanctuary.

2012-04-29.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-04-29.abstract: Top magazine and long-form articles this week.

2012-04-29.title: Protesters in Malaysia Accuse Police of Brutality
2012-04-29.abstract: A group calling for electoral changes said the police beat protesters and used water cannons and tear gas to break up a weekend rally, and it demanded an inquiry.

2012-04-29.title: U.S. Drone Strike Underlines Clash of Interests in Pakistan
2012-04-29.abstract: With officials from the two countries trying to reset badly stalled relations, an American attack killed three suspected militants in Pakistan’s tribal belt.

2012-04-30.title: A Race Recast by YouTube and Twitter
2012-04-30.abstract: A law passed in 2007 that was intended to keep campaigning orderly and clean has been undercut by the unpredictable and uncontrollable Web.

2012-04-30.title: Sudan Declares State of Emergency as Clashes Continue
2012-04-30.abstract: Sudanese authorities along the border with South Sudan will have wide powers to make arrests and set up special courts.

2012-04-30.title: U.N. Chief Will Ask West to Lift Myanmar Sanctions
2012-04-30.abstract: As Ban Ki-moon, the United Nations secretary general, addresses the nation’s Parliament, he is expected to call on Western nations to revisit their positions and to offer aid.

2012-04-30.title: Philippines Role May Expand as U.S. Adjusts Asia Strategy
2012-04-30.abstract: The American military presence in the Philippines has grown recently amid fear of a rising China, and top American and Philippine officials are meeting to discuss further buildup.

2012-04-30.title: Former Israeli Premier Assails Netanyahu on Iran
2012-04-30.abstract: In a wide-ranging critique of Benjamin Netanyahu that drew boos at a New York conference, Ehud Olmert urged him not to rush into military action against Iran.
